reason:
Agent attempted to use GitHub tool to retrieve authenticated username, but the GitHub OAuth token retrieval failed with a 502 error, preventing the verification of a GitHub username in the response.
  Bugs found:
    - GitHub OAuth Token Retrieval Failed - The agent successfully executed the GitHub tool request and permission flow, but when attempting to fetch the authenticated GitHub user information, the proxy returned a 502 "Failed to fetch access token" error. The response message states: "The GitHub OAuth token cannot be retrieved. This appears to be a problem with the connected account itself." The agent worked for 40 seconds and executed multiple Bash commands (Fetch authenticated GitHub user, Raw GitHub user response, Check connected accounts metadata, Verbose curl to GitHub API), but all failed due to the token retrieval error. The response does NOT include a GitHub username as expected.
steps: Navigated to http://localhost:47891 — Page loaded successfully with sidebar showing agents including "QA-20260506-001150-gdur" with "idle" status.
Clicked on agent "QA-20260506-001150-gdur" in sidebar — Agent page opened, showing agent status as "idle" in the header and a message input field.
Verified agent status is "idle" — Status confirmed as "idle" in the agent header.
Typed message "Use the GitHub tool to check who I am. Tell me my GitHub username." into input field — Message successfully entered in the message composer.
Clicked "Send message" button — Message sent successfully, agent status changed to "working", and a new session "GitHub Identity Verification Session" was created with "working" status.
Waited 3 seconds for response — Agent continued processing; a "Request Connected Account" card appeared asking to grant GitHub account access.

Clicked "Allow Access (1)" button on GitHub account access card — GitHub access was granted, agent status returned to "working" and a new "Bash" tool card appeared showing "Fetch authenticated GitHub user".
Waited 5 seconds for further response — An "API Request Review" card appeared asking to allow a "GET /user" request to the GitHub API.
Clicked "Allow" button on API request review card — Permission dialog appeared with scope options (Allow Once, Always allow user, Always allow read:user, Always allow all github requests).
Selected "Always allow read:user" permission option — Permission was accepted, agent continued processing with status "working" and Bash tool executing.

Waited 8 seconds for agent response completion — Agent completed execution after 40 seconds. Agent status changed to "idle". Agent returned error message: "The proxy is returning 502 'Failed to fetch access token' — the GitHub OAuth token cannot be retrieved. This appears to be a problem with the connected account itself." Multiple Bash tool cards showed completed status but the final response was an error, NOT a GitHub username.
Took final screenshot — Screenshot captured showing the agent's error response instead of the expected GitHub username.
bugs: GitHub OAuth Token Retrieval Failed - The agent successfully executed the GitHub tool request and permission flow, but when attempting to fetch the authenticated GitHub user information, the proxy returned a 502 "Failed to fetch access token" error. The response message states: "The GitHub OAuth token cannot be retrieved. This appears to be a problem with the connected account itself." The agent worked for 40 seconds and executed multiple Bash commands (Fetch authenticated GitHub user, Raw GitHub user response, Check connected accounts metadata, Verbose curl to GitHub API), but all failed due to the token retrieval error. The response does NOT include a GitHub username as expected.
